summary: Justin Brennan conducts a Q&A mentorship session covering multifamily syndication mechanics, deal analyzer usage, financing structures, and reserves. While mostly focused on general syndication education, the content includes deal underwriting tactics relevant to Brian's drag areas of deal sourcing and underwriting.
takeaways: Investors receive 60-70% of deal returns split amongst them; pay out quarterly from cash flow
Cost segregation study completed within 1-2 months of closing; if doing renovations, inform cost seg company to include them in depreciation
Bonus depreciation squeezes 39-year commercial property depreciation into 5 years, with majority received in year one
If you've raised at least 50% of required capital by end of due diligence period, proceed with deal close
Close with down payment + closing costs only (typically needed at closing); can raise remaining capital including renovation budget post-close within 30 days
Replacement reserves: $250-300 per unit per year built into annual operating expenses; add extra 10-15% on renovation budget as starting reserve capital to avoid under-capitalization
Light renovations: $3,500-5,500 per unit; medium renovations: $7,000-10,000 per unit; heavy renovations: $14,000-25,000 per unit
Seller financing typically 1-1.1% closing costs; match term to first loan (ideally 5 years); seller retains any accumulated reserve account at sale exit

skill_candidates.change: Improve underwriting precision by properly capitalizing deals with separate starting reserve account
skill_candidates.evidence: Justin emphasizes adding 10-15% on top of renovation budget as starting reserve capital to avoid under-capitalization: 'I would rather have more money and be able to give money back than to not have enough money'

skill_candidates.change: Optimize closing timeline by understanding minimum capital requirements vs. total equity raise needed
skill_candidates.evidence: Justin teaches that with $750K equity need and only $600K raised, close if you have down payment + closing costs (e.g., $625K), then raise remaining $125K post-close within 30 days

skill_candidates.change: Accurately project operating expenses by budgeting unit-level renovation costs based on scope
skill_candidates.evidence: Justin provides specific ranges: light $3,500-5,500/unit; medium $7,000-10,000/unit; heavy $14,000-25,000/unit, with 80-unit example at $25K/unit

title: Tuesday Mentorship Q&A - Building Deal Momentum Now - 01/06/26 | Justin Brennan
